page.title: Inversion Basics
page.subtitle_html: Inversion Basics — B2 inversion after negative or limiting adverbials and not only patterns.
page.subtitle_text: B2 inversion after negative or limiting adverbials and not only patterns.

blocks.body: {"level":"B2","intro":"Inversion Basics: Inversion Basics explains B2 word order patterns such as never, rarely, only after, not until, and not only.","rules":[{"label":"Negative adverbials","text":"After never, rarely, seldom, or hardly, use auxiliary inversion.","example":"Never have I seen such a result."},{"label":"Only phrases","text":"After only + time or condition at the front, invert the main clause.","example":"Only after the meeting did we understand."},{"label":"Not only","text":"Use inversion after not only, then continue with but also.","example":"Not only did he apologize, but he also helped."}]}

blocks.heading: B2 focus
blocks.body: This compact page exists as the theory anchor for the Polyglot English B2 lesson package.
